composer_signature.matcher: footer status row matches (?:minimal|low|medium|high|xhigh)\s+·\s AND an input row (at/above it) begins with '›' — the '›' glyph alone is shared with codex selection menus (incl. trust modal), so the footer middot+cwd is the disambiguator
composer_signature.welcome_banner_note: 'OpenAI Codex (vX)' box is present at fresh launch but scrolls off after the first submitted message (no alt-screen), so it is NOT a durable composer anchor — the footer status line is.

healthy_approved_suggestion.note: The word approved is composer ghost/example text; the active footer proves this is the healthy composer, not a modal.

phase_a_default_mode.induced_by: prompt 'Run this exact shell command: echo probe-ok'
final_rows: Tip: New Use /fast to enable our fastest inference with increased plan usage.
› Run this exact shell command: echo probe-ok
• You have 4 usage limit resets available. Run /usage to use one.
⚠ MCP startup interrupted. The following servers were not initialized: codex_apps
• Running the requested shell command exactly as given.

phase_b_untrusted_mode.matcher: an approval modal shows a 'Would you like to run the following command?' header, a '$ <cmd>' line, and a numbered option list with the '›' glyph on the selected row ('› 1. Yes, proceed (y)' / 2. Yes+don't-ask / 3. No (esc)), plus a 'Press enter to confirm or esc to cancel' footer. The composer footer status line is NOT present while the modal owns the screen — its absence + this option list is the readiness-gate 'blocked' signal
modal_rows: › Run this exact shell command: touch probe-approval-check.txt
Would you like to run the following command?
2. Yes, and don't ask again for commands that start with `touch probe-approval-check.txt` (p)
3. No, and tell Codex what to do differently (esc)
Press enter to confirm or esc to cancel

modal_mode_sequences.note: scan of the last ~12KB of raw frames spanning the modal render; codex uses NO alt-screen (?1049 absent) — the modal is drawn inline over the main screen via direct cursor addressing + ?2026 sync

evidence.post_submit_composer: › Find and fix a bug in @filename

newline_sequence.note: codex pushes the kitty keyboard protocol (ESC[>7u) at startup, so Shift+Enter is expected to arrive as ESC[13;2u; Ctrl-J (0x0A) is the classic newline-without-submit. Winner = first chord that inserted a newline without submitting.

honors_bracketed_paste.evidence: ESC[?2004h present in raw startup log

paste_multiline_semantics.conclusion: held as one multiline composer entry; trailing CR submits

mid_generation_inject.mid_stream_semantics: buffered — text landed in composer as an unsent draft; the trailing CR did NOT submit while a turn was active
final_nonempty_tail: esc again to edit previous message

graceful_exit_sequence.observed: graceful_close terminated the child via: Ctrl-C
graceful_exit_sequence.note: single Ctrl-C exits from an idle empty composer (verified in P1 x2). With text in the composer the first Ctrl-C clears it, so send up to two. On exit codex pops the kitty keyboard flags (ESC[<u) and shows a brief 'Shutting down...' line — no alt-screen to restore.
